attributes.mlflow.spanInputs: {"question": "Which genre of Italian film was the first to use CGI?"}
attributes.mlflow.spanOutputs: {"trajectory": {"thought_0": "I need to find out which genre of Italian film was the first to use CGI. This may require looking up information about the history of CGI in Italian cinema.", "tool_name_0": "search_wikipedia", "tool_args_0": {"query": "first Italian film genre to use CGI"}, "observation_0": ["The Stendhal Syndrome | The Stendhal Syndrome \"(Ital. La Sindrome di Stendhal)\" is a 1996 Italian horror film written and directed by Dario Argento and starring his daughter Asia Argento. It was the first Italian film to use computer-generated imagery (CGI).", "Azur & Asmar: The Princes' Quest | Azur & Asmar: The Princes' Quest (French: Azur et Asmar ) is a 2006 French-Spanish-Belgium-Italian 3D CGI animated fairytale fantasy film written and directed by Michel Ocelot and animated at the Paris animation and visual effects studio Mac Guff Ligne. It is Ocelot's fourth feature, though his first wholly original creation since \"Kirikou and the Sorceress\", and his first use of 3D computer graphics, albeit an atypical employment of this medium with two-dimensional, painted backgrounds and non-photorealistic rendering. Like most of his films it is an original fairy tale, in this case inspired by the folklore (such as the \"One Thousand and One Nights\") and decorative art of North Africa and the Middle East and with an increased degree of characterisation relative to his previous works which pushes it into the genre of fairytale fantasy.", "Gladiators of Rome (2012 film) | Gladiators of Rome (Italian: \"Gladiatori di Roma\"), also known as Not Born to Be Gladiators and referred to in marketing as Gladiators of Rome 3D, is a 2012 Italian computer-animated comedy film produced by Rainbow CGI and released by Medusa Film. It is directed by Iginio Straffi, and was opened in Italy on October 2012 and was later released across Europe and the Americas. Produced on a budget of $45 million, it is one of the most expensive Italian films ever made. The film grossed between $10 million and $50 million worldwide."], "thought_1": "The first Italian film to use CGI is \"The Stendhal Syndrome,\" a horror film directed by Dario Argento. I now have the information needed to answer the question.", "tool_name_1": "finish", "tool_args_1": {}, "observation_1": "Completed."}, "reasoning": "The first Italian film to use computer-generated imagery (CGI) is \"The Stendhal Syndrome,\" a horror film directed by Dario Argento, released in 1996. This film marked a significant milestone in the use of CGI within the Italian film industry.", "answer": "The first genre of Italian film to use CGI is horror, specifically with the film \"The Stendhal Syndrome.\""}

attributes.name: "search_wikipedia"
attributes.mlflow.spanInputs: {"query": "first Italian film genre to use CGI"}
attributes.mlflow.spanOutputs: ["The Stendhal Syndrome | The Stendhal Syndrome \"(Ital. La Sindrome di Stendhal)\" is a 1996 Italian horror film written and directed by Dario Argento and starring his daughter Asia Argento. It was the first Italian film to use computer-generated imagery (CGI).", "Azur & Asmar: The Princes' Quest | Azur & Asmar: The Princes' Quest (French: Azur et Asmar ) is a 2006 French-Spanish-Belgium-Italian 3D CGI animated fairytale fantasy film written and directed by Michel Ocelot and animated at the Paris animation and visual effects studio Mac Guff Ligne. It is Ocelot's fourth feature, though his first wholly original creation since \"Kirikou and the Sorceress\", and his first use of 3D computer graphics, albeit an atypical employment of this medium with two-dimensional, painted backgrounds and non-photorealistic rendering. Like most of his films it is an original fairy tale, in this case inspired by the folklore (such as the \"One Thousand and One Nights\") and decorative art of North Africa and the Middle East and with an increased degree of characterisation relative to his previous works which pushes it into the genre of fairytale fantasy.", "Gladiators of Rome (2012 film) | Gladiators of Rome (Italian: \"Gladiatori di Roma\"), also known as Not Born to Be Gladiators and referred to in marketing as Gladiators of Rome 3D, is a 2012 Italian computer-animated comedy film produced by Rainbow CGI and released by Medusa Film. It is directed by Iginio Straffi, and was opened in Italy on October 2012 and was later released across Europe and the Americas. Produced on a budget of $45 million, it is one of the most expensive Italian films ever made. The film grossed between $10 million and $50 million worldwide."]

attributes.mlflow.spanInputs:
{"question": "Which genre of Italian film was the first to use CGI?", "trajectory": "[[ ## thought_0 ## ]]
I need to find out which genre of Italian film was the first to use CGI. This may require looking up information about the history of CGI in Italian cinema.

[[ ## tool_name_0 ## ]]
search_wikipedia

[[ ## tool_args_0 ## ]]
{"query": "first Italian film genre to use CGI"}

[[ ## observation_0 ## ]]
[1] «The Stendhal Syndrome | The Stendhal Syndrome "(Ital. La Sindrome di Stendhal)" is a 1996 Italian horror film written and directed by Dario Argento and starring his daughter Asia Argento. It was the first Italian film to use computer-generated imagery (CGI).»
[2] «Azur & Asmar: The Princes' Quest | Azur & Asmar: The Princes' Quest (French: Azur et Asmar ) is a 2006 French-Spanish-Belgium-Italian 3D CGI animated fairytale fantasy film written and directed by Michel Ocelot and animated at the Paris animation and visual effects studio Mac Guff Ligne. It is Ocelot's fourth feature, though his first wholly original creation since "Kirikou and the Sorceress", and his first use of 3D computer graphics, albeit an atypical employment of this medium with two-dimensional, painted backgrounds and non-photorealistic rendering. Like most of his films it is an original fairy tale, in this case inspired by the folklore (such as the "One Thousand and One Nights") and decorative art of North Africa and the Middle East and with an increased degree of characterisation relative to his previous works which pushes it into the genre of fairytale fantasy.»
[3] «Gladiators of Rome (2012 film) | Gladiators of Rome (Italian: "Gladiatori di Roma"), also known as Not Born to Be Gladiators and referred to in marketing as Gladiators of Rome 3D, is a 2012 Italian computer-animated comedy film produced by Rainbow CGI and released by Medusa Film. It is directed by Iginio Straffi, and was opened in Italy on October 2012 and was later released across Europe and the Americas. Produced on a budget of $45 million, it is one of the most expensive Italian films ever made. The film grossed between $10 million and $50 million worldwide.»"}

Like most of his films it is an original fairy tale, in this case inspired by the folklore (such as the "One Thousand and One Nights") and decorative art of North Africa and the Middle East and with an increased degree of characterisation relative to his previous works which pushes it into the genre of fairytale fantasy.»
[3] «Gladiators of Rome (2012 film) | Gladiators of Rome (Italian: "Gladiatori di Roma"), also known as Not Born to Be Gladiators and referred to in marketing as Gladiators of Rome 3D, is a 2012 Italian computer-animated comedy film produced by Rainbow CGI and released by Medusa Film. It is directed by Iginio Straffi, and was opened in Italy on October 2012 and was later released across Europe and the Americas. Produced on a budget of $45 million, it is one of the most expensive Italian films ever made. The film grossed between $10 million and $50 million worldwide.»
